text.title: Tool Trust Boundary Model Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tool Trust Boundary Model application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every tool is classified hard-wall (blocked from untrusted input) / soft-wall (confirmable) / observable (logged); no fourth class.

rationale.source: Tool Trust Boundary Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Open enums of trust classes lead to inconsistent gating across teams.

rule.statement: Untrusted content is wrapped as data, never spliced into instruction context; markers identify origin.

rationale.source: Tool Trust Boundary Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Inline splicing is the root cause of indirect prompt injection; wrapping forces the model to treat the content as content.

rule.statement: Hard-wall tools (delete / send-funds / publish-public) require explicit human confirmation per call, not blanket consent.

rationale.source: Tool Trust Boundary Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Blanket consent erodes within days; per-call confirmation matches the cost of the action.

rule.statement: External-write tools carry a per-agent rate limit + circuit breaker; exceed limit pages on-call.

rationale.source: Tool Trust Boundary Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Compromised agents fan out via external writes; rate limit + circuit breaker are the cheap blast-radius cap.

rule.statement: Every tool call logs: agent id, caller surface, input source taxonomy tag, output shape; logs retained ≥90 days.

rationale.source: Tool Trust Boundary Model authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Without audit trail, post-incident forensics fail; 90 days matches typical regulatory floor.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
